name: La Camocha Greenway and Llantones River Trail
name_original: Senda Verde de La Camocha en Gijón volviendo por la Senda Fluvial del Río Llantones
description: Easy 9.8-mile touring cycling route through riverside forests and meadows, featuring historical mining sites near Gijón.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Gijón/Xixón offers a diverse landscape, blending urban areas with extensive natural features. The region features a network of greenways, coastal paths, and river trails, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Riders can explore flat routes along the coast, gentle rural hills, or more challenging ascents in areas like Mount Deva. This topography allows for a wide range of cycling experiences, from leisurely rides to demanding routes. Best touring cycling routes around Gijón/Xixón The most popular touring cycling route is La Camocha Greenway and Piles River Path Loop, a 14.7 miles (23.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines a repurposed mining train line with a scenic river path. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Gijón, Cabo Peñas, Luanco, Candás — circular by the coast, a difficult 47.2 miles (75.9 km) path. This route offers extensive coastal views and passes through charming towns. Local touring cyclists also love the La Camocha Greenway and Llantones River Trail, a 9.7 miles (15.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and along a river, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Gijón/Xixón is defined by coastal paths, greenways, and rural hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside rides to difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Gijón/Xixón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 350 reviews. More than 5000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Gijón/Xixón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Gijón/Xixón?
A: There are over 150 touring cycling routes around Gijón/Xixón, offering a wide variety of experiences from coastal paths to rural greenways and more challenging ascents. This diverse network caters to all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Gijón/Xixón offers a varied topography for touring cyclists. You can find flat routes along the coast, gentle hills in rural areas, and more demanding mountain passes just outside the city, such as those around Mount Deva, Pico Fario, and Alto de Fumarea. Greenways and river paths provide smoother, more leisurely options.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Gijón/Xixón has several family-friendly options. The city's greenways and river paths, like the Piles River Path or the Ñora River Path, are excellent for leisurely rides. Routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048920986/la-camocha-greenway-and-llantones-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Camocha Greenway and Llantones River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, rated as easy, are suitable for families looking for a relaxed cycling experience through rural landscapes.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by notable landmarks. Along coastal paths, you can enjoy views of the Cantabrian Sea and iconic spots like San Lorenzo Beach. The Cervigón Path features sculptures and viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Cervigón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/473306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Providencia Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Within the city, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5042924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Marqués de San Esteban&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive Universidad Laboral.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Gijón/Xixón?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029986607/la-camocha-greenway-and-piles-river-path-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Camocha Greenway and Piles River Path Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines a repurposed mining train line with a scenic river path. For a more challenging coastal experience, consider the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042720208/gijon-cabo-penas-luanco-candas-circular-by-the-coast" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gijón, Cabo Peñas, Luanco, Candás — circular by the coast&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Gijón/Xixón offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. The region benefits from a mild climate. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and less crowded routes. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal rides, though it can be warmer. Winter cycling is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potential rain.

Q: Are there options for advanced touring cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Absolutely. While there are many easy and moderate routes, Gijón/Xixón also offers difficult touring cycling routes with significant elevation gains. Examples include routes that venture into the rural and mountainous terrain surrounding the city, such as those leading to Pico Fario or Alto de Fumarea, or the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Gijón – Church of San Pedro loop&lt;/a&gt; with over 900 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find bike-friendly cafes or places to stop along the routes?
A: Many routes, especially those closer to urban areas or passing through towns, will have opportunities to stop for refreshments. The city of Gijón/Xixón itself has a vibrant culinary scene, and smaller villages along greenways or coastal paths often feature cafes and restaurants where cyclists can take a break and refuel.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Gijón/Xixón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 350 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscape, which allows for a mix of coastal views, river paths, and rural scenery. The well-maintained greenways and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is public transport accessible for cyclists in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Gijón/Xixón has a commitment to cycling, which includes an updated network of bike lanes. While specific rules for bikes on public transport can vary, the city's infrastructure makes it accessible to reach many trailheads by bike or to combine cycling with short public transport links. It's advisable to check local transport regulations for carrying bikes on buses or trains.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly touring cycling trails?
A: While many natural areas are generally welcoming, specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary. River paths and greenways are often suitable for cycling with dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and you adhere to local guidelines. One route that passes by a dog park is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1079316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Siero Dog Park – Río Nora Riverside Trail loop&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: A pedestrian and cycling route that runs along an old mining railway line and that passes through forests, rivers and meadows. It follows a flat, paved route, with several rest areas and viewpoints along the way. The route also passes through several towns and villages, allowing you to learn about the history and culture of the region.

The Vía Verde follows a good part of the route of the mining railway that connected the wells of La Camocha with Veriña. It goes through a long illuminated tunnel that passes under the Carbonera road.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for all cycling levels?
faqs.answer: Yes, the La Camocha Greenway and Llantones River Trail is rated as "easy" and is suitable for all cycling levels, including families. Its origin as a former mining railway ensures a gentle, consistent gradient, making it very accessible.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of paved sections, compacted limestone gravel, and asphalt, providing a generally smooth ride. While some unpaved sections may be encountered, they are typically manageable for touring bicycles.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse scenery including riverside forests, meadows, and rural areas. Notable features include passage along the Pilón stream, wooden bridges, and views of the Aliseda Pantanosa. You'll also pass by the ruins of the 12th-century Santa María de Leorio parish church and see the towering headframes of the former La Camocha mine, an important industrial heritage site. Don't miss the La Camocha Greenway itself and the Tunnel on the Vía Verde de La Camocha.

faqs.question: How do I access the trailhead and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail is well-integrated into Gijón's existing network of cycle lanes, facilitating easy access from the city center. While specific parking details aren't provided, its accessibility suggests various options within the Gijón/Xixón municipality. Look for parking near the start of the La Camocha Greenway.

faqs.question: Is the La Camocha Greenway and Llantones River Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, allowing you to bring your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in more populated areas or near wildlife, to ensure a safe and enjoyable experience for everyone.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summer is also a good option, though it can be warmer. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy the La Camocha Greenway and Llantones River Trail. It is a public recreational route.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is designed as a circular trail, allowing you to start and end at the same point without needing to retrace your steps. The complete circular route spans approximately 24 km, with the core greenway and river path sections being around 15.7 km.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates significant sections of the Senda verde de La Camocha and the Senda fluvial del Rio Llantones. You may also encounter smaller segments of paths like the Camín de Casielles or the Vía de la Plata.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or rest areas along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers designated rest areas that provide scenic views, particularly of the Llantones valley. While specific amenities like cafes are not detailed for the trail itself, its proximity to Gijón means facilities are easily accessible nearby.

faqs.question: What makes this trail particularly good for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: The trail's origin as a repurposed mining railway line is a key advantage for touring cyclists. This historical transformation means it boasts a largely flat and accessible design with minimal elevation changes, making for a comfortable and enjoyable ride. It's an excellent example of a 'greenway' (vía verde) promoting sustainable exploration.

waypoints.name: Tunnel on the Vía Verde de La Camocha
